accept.reason: Repo-wide git-history churn / temporal-coupling. Advisory only and not code-fixable in source; the wave's hotspot lane mines whole-repo history and ignores --source-prefix, so it cannot be driven to zero by editing skill code.

Residual complexity after decomposing EVERY CC>40 god-function (lint() 407->17, validate_scheduler_ledger 127->17, validate_branch_status validators, render_branch_worktree main 150, summarize_telemetry, create_runtime_packet/runtime_packet_runner, reconcile_goal_run, check_goal_config, amendment_lib, validate_main_status, promote, extract_telemetry, scheduler_tick). Max CC is now <=40. The remaining CC 11-40 + function_nloc + parameter_count + MI findings are the irreducible idiom floor: pervasive defensive `x.get() if isinstance(x.get(),dict) else {}` guards (each counted as a branch) and the explicit keyword-threaded helpers produced by the decompositions. Advisory (Tier-2).

Irreducible long single-line string literals after `ruff format` (line-length 120): prompt/manifest templates, raise/defect error-message f-strings, and route-policy text. Wrapping would split string contents (changing generated output) for zero behavior value.
Three if/else blocks left explicit because the ternary rewrite produces hard-to-read nested/mixed-precedence expressions; readability over the lint nit.

Standalone-script idiom by design. The skill scripts are intentionally self-contained: generated 36-line importlib wrapper shims, the per-script _load_module/sys.path bootstrap, and the read_json/write_json/sha256_file helpers are duplicated so each script runs standalone; the three runtime runners are deliberately decoupled. Genuine logic clones were extracted (source_record/add_if_exists -> amendment_lib); the rest must stay duplicated to preserve the standalone design.

Linter base-path false positives: references resolve bundle-relative / skill-relative (e.g. amendments/, lite/, scripts/..., ../../_goal_shared/...) and do not resolve from the repo root, but all targets exist. The standalone docs-consistency wave lane reports 0.
Standalone-script sibling imports (e.g. amendment_lib, render_bootloader) resolved at runtime via the per-script sys.path bootstrap; not missing PyPI dependencies.
ruff/pyright are dev CLI tools declared in requirements-dev.txt / devDependencies and invoked as commands, not imported as modules; AST import scanning cannot see them.
False positive: bandit matches the string literals "pass"/"partial" (run/branch status outcome keys and sentinel values) as hardcoded passwords. No credentials are present anywhere in the package.

By design: deterministic subprocess launches of git / codex / the opencode-worker-bridge control script, always list-form argv with shell=False; base_ref and other interpolated values are validated (validate_base_ref). This is a CLI orchestration tool whose purpose is launching those processes.
By design: partial executable paths (git, codex, opencode_worker.py) are resolved via shutil.which / known skill roots; list-form, no shell. Expected for a CLI orchestration tool.
By design: `import subprocess` is required - this package's function is to launch and supervise model/CLI worker processes.
By design: try/except/continue in tolerant artifact/ledger parsers, intentionally skipping malformed entries rather than crashing the orchestrator.
By design: os.chmod(launch.sh, 0o755) makes the generated launcher script executable; world-writable bit is not set.

By design: /dev/shm is used as a faster scratch root ONLY when writable (mkdir + exist_ok, writability-gated) with a packet-local fallback; it is a directory root, not a predictable-filename temp file (CWE-377 does not apply).

False positive: bandit B101 (assert_used) flags pytest assertions in the repo's own regression suite (tests/test_bug_classes.py, tests/test_owned_paths_guard.py). assert is pytest's assertion mechanism, not an -O-stripped runtime guard. The diagnosis wave already excludes tests/ from source lanes by default; the bandit security lane is the only leaf that reaches into it.

Archived audit-report artifacts (a captured git-worktree snapshot under maintenance/reports/toyoptimization-*/), not package source. The repo's own ruff config already sets extend-exclude=["maintenance/reports"]; bandit B101 (assert_used) hits inside the snapshot's test_toyopt.py are doubly out of scope.
